2017-06-25.abstract: At least 100 others were seriously injured after people rushed to the overturned tanker to collect spilled fuel and it caught fire, officials said.

2017-06-25.title: Global News Quiz: King Salman Shocker, Roman Misbehavior and Indian Sedition
2017-06-25.abstract: Did you stay on top of the most important stories last week from around the world? Test your knowledge of international events with our quick quiz, recapping major news headlines from across the globe. To take the quiz, click on an answer, and the correct response will be revealed after you choose.

2017-06-26.title: Italian Runoff Elections Boost Center-Right Parties
2017-06-26.abstract: According to official results, candidates representing the anti-immigrant Northern League and other rightist parties won many of the major municipal races.

2017-06-26.title: Liu Xiaobo, Chinese Nobel Laureate, Leaves Prison for Cancer Care
2017-06-26.abstract: Mr. Liu, who was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2010 for his writings promoting democracy, has late-stage liver cancer, his lawyers said.

2017-06-26.title: Trump’s Anti-Nafta Stance Is on a Collision Course with Natural Gas
2017-06-26.abstract: Mexico’s economy and U.S. energy companies depend on free-flowing cross-border natural gas. Both are scrambling to avoid the chaotic disruption of a trade war.

2017-06-26.title: Ex-C.I.A. Chief Stirs War Debate in Australia. Also: How Much Is the Great Barrier Reef Worth?
2017-06-26.abstract: Conversation starters and context, drawn from the day’s news in Australia.

2017-06-26.title: Theresa May Reaches Deal for Backing of Northern Ireland Party
2017-06-26.abstract: The support of the Democratic Unionists will keep the prime minister and the Conservatives in power, at least for now.

2017-06-27.title: Few Overseas Have Faith in Trump’s Leadership, Survey Finds
2017-06-27.abstract: A Pew Research Center poll found that President Trump and his policies are broadly unpopular around the world, especially among some of America’s closest allies.

2017-06-27.title: Trump Meets India’s Leader, a Fellow Nationalist Battling China for His Favor
2017-06-27.abstract: The display of warmth between President Trump and Prime Minister Narendra Modi was at least partly aimed at China, which has disappointed Mr. Trump in recent weeks.

2017-06-27.title: President Michel Temer of Brazil Is Charged With Corruption
2017-06-27.abstract: Days after a poll showed his approval rating at 7 percent, the lowest for a Brazilian president in nearly 30 years, Mr. Temer was accused of taking a $152,000 bribe.

2017-06-27.title: U.N. Brought Cholera to Haiti. Now It Is Fumbling Its Effort to Atone.
2017-06-27.abstract: The trust fund to finance the U.N.’s anti-cholera plan is nearly broke. Members are balking at paying. And a class-action lawsuit continued to dog the U.N.

2017-06-27.title: Australia, Diverse and Graying. Also: Johnny Depp, and a Controversial Push to Decrypt
2017-06-27.abstract: Conversation starters and context, drawn from the day’s news in Australia.

2017-06-28.title: Trump Welcomes South Korean Leader as Options on the North Wane
2017-06-28.abstract: President Trump will try to win over South Korea’s progressive new leader, Moon Jae-in, who is more interested in engaging with the North than Mr. Trump’s advisers.

2017-06-28.title: Alain Senderens, a Chef Who Modernized French Food, Dies at 77
2017-06-28.abstract: Mr. Senderens, one of the most adventurous of the founding fathers of nouvelle cuisine, produced dishes that could entice and, on occasion, shock.

2017-06-28.title: Colombian Anticorruption Official Is Arrested in U.S. Bribery Case
2017-06-28.abstract: The director of Colombia’s anticorruption unit was arrested in his country’s capital after D.E.A. agents in Miami recorded him at meetings where bribes were discussed.

2017-06-28.title: Police Officers in Helicopter Attack Venezuela’s Supreme Court
2017-06-28.abstract: The attack, with grenades, was a rare act of disloyalty against a government under pressure from protests and an economic collapse.

2017-06-28.title: Fighting Anti-Feminist Trolls. Also: Ransomware Victimhood, and a Dramatic Citizen’s Arrest.
2017-06-28.abstract: Conversation starters and context, drawn from the day’s news in Australia.

2017-06-29.title: Rogue Police Copter Attacks Venezuela’s Supreme Court
2017-06-29.abstract: A rogue police force attacked Venezuela's Supreme Court in Caracas by helicopter on Tuesday, amid antigovernment protests.

2017-06-29.title: After Helicopter Attack, Venezuelans Ask, What Was That About?
2017-06-29.abstract: If the assault on government buildings was meant as a call to arms, it instead bewildered Venezuelans who have been protesting the government for months.

2017-06-29.title: Australian Cardinal and Aide to Pope Is Charged With Sexual Assault
2017-06-29.abstract: Cardinal George Pell, the country’s most senior Roman Catholic prelate and the Vatican’s de facto finance chief, has been ordered to appear in court next month.

2017-06-29.title: That Low Hum of Racism
2017-06-29.abstract: We explore race and the Indigenous experience in this week’s Australia Letter. Plus, a new feature called The Breakdown.

2017-06-29.title: Trump’s R.S.V.P. to Macron: Yes to Bastille Day in Paris
2017-06-29.abstract: When the White House asked if the trip would be worth Mr. Trump’s time, the French president said it would be a powerful symbol of trans-Atlantic unity.

2017-06-30.title: Report Confirms That Chemical Arms Were Used on Syrian Villagers
2017-06-30.abstract: The Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons concluded that the nerve agent sarin or a similar poison had been used in the April 4 attack.

2017-06-30.title: U.N. to Reduce Peacekeepers in Darfur Region of Sudan
2017-06-30.abstract: The Security Council voted to withdraw more than a third of the nearly 19,000 peacekeepers in the volatile region.

2017-06-30.title: Edward Kline, ‘Silent Partner’ in Aiding Soviet Dissidents, Dies at 85
2017-06-30.abstract: Mr. Kline helped to shepherd Andrei D. Sakharov’s memoirs into worldwide distribution and set up a publishing house in New York to print books that were banned by the Soviet authorities.

2017-06-30.title: A Meeting of Military Might in the Pacific. Also: Tony Abbott Defends Cardinal Pell (Again).
2017-06-30.abstract: Conversation starters and context, drawn from the day’s news in Australia.

2017-06-30.title: German Parliament Approves Same-Sex Marriage
2017-06-30.abstract: Lawmakers supported the bill after Chancellor Angela Merkel softened her resistance to the issue a few days ago.
